title: CV Fundamentals
subtitle: Image tensors, OpenCV basics, convolutions

exercises.title: Implement gaussian_blur
hints: cv2.getGaussianKernel(k, 0) returns a (k, 1) 1-D kernel that sums to 1. Outer-product it with its transpose to get the 2-D kernel: kernel_1d @ kernel_1d.T.
cv2.filter2D(image, -1, kernel) does the convolution. The -1 tells OpenCV to keep the input dtype (uint8 in / uint8 out).

exercises.title: Sharpen with a custom convolution kernel
hints: A sharpen kernel emphasises the centre pixel and subtracts its neighbours. The classic 3x3 is [[0,-1,0],[-1,5,-1],[0,-1,0]] — note it sums to 1, so flat regions keep their brightness while edges get boosted.
Build the kernel with np.array(..., dtype=np.float32) and convolve in one call: cv2.filter2D(image, -1, kernel). The -1 keeps the input dtype (uint8 in / uint8 out).

exercises.title: Tune Canny thresholds on edge detection
self_checks: Saw the 'snowstorm' of false edges when both thresholds are too low
Kept high_threshold ≈ 2–3× low_threshold and noted the effect

self_checks_global: I understand image tensor shapes (C,H,W) vs (H,W,C)
I read the OpenCV filter2D docs
